title: Assessment of Cas9 Activity in Cas9 Transduced Cancer Cell Lines
author: Verity Goodwin
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bgvtjw6n
materials: If cells are >90% confluent, passage each well:
Follow "Fixing cell pellets" protocol found here: https://protocols.io/view/fixing-cell-pellets-bg2fjybn.html

steps.title: Detach and collect cas9 cells as...
steps.instruction: Detach and collect cas9 cells as per protocol "Passaging adherent cancer cell lines" steps 1-8 found here: https://protocols.io/view/passaging-adherent-cancer-cell-lines-bgtbjwin.html

steps.title: Prepare a cell suspension containing 1x10^6 cells in 7
steps.instruction: Prepare a cell suspension containing 1x10^6 cells in 7.2 mL media (this equates to 2.5x10^6 cells per well in 1.8 mL .

steps.title: Prepare a cell suspension containing 1x10 6 cells in 7
steps.instruction: Prepare a cell suspension containing 1x10 6 cells in 7.2 mL media (this equates to 2.5x10 5 cells per well in 1.8 mL ).

steps.title: Add 6
steps.instruction: Add 6.4 µL polybrene (8µg/ml concentration) to the cell suspension and mix using a 10ml stripette and pipetboy.

steps.title: Transfer 1
steps.instruction: Transfer 1.8 mL of the cell suspension into 3 wells of a 6 well plate to include a mock, control, and reporter transduction with the final cell number and reagent volumes shown in table 1. Ensure the plate is clearly labelled as per Figure 1. %s %s WellTransfectionNumber of CellsVolume of PolybreneCell suspensionVector1Mock2.5 x 1051.6µl1.8ml200ul media2Control2.5 x 1051.6µl1.8ml200ul BFP/GFP3Reporter2.5 x 1051.6µl1.8ml200ul BFP/GFP/gGFP Figure 1. 6 well plate layout

steps.title: Add 200 µL of media to...
steps.instruction: Add 200 µL of media to the mock well, and 200 µL of the designated control or reporter virus to the appropriate well (see table 1). Mix well by rocking the plate gently.

steps.title: Place the plate in the incubator
steps.instruction: Place the plate in the incubator.

steps.title: if cells are <90% confluent, carefully...
steps.instruction: if cells are <90% confluent, carefully replace media with 2 mL fresh media per well, without dislodging the cells.

steps.title: Aspirate culture media from each well,...
steps.instruction: Aspirate culture media from each well, and carefully wash each well with 2 mL PBS by angling the pipette at the side of the wells. Aspirate PBS.

steps.title: Add 500 µL TrypLE to each...
steps.instruction: Add 500 µL TrypLE to each well and place in the incubator for 3-5 minutes. Use the microscope if necessary to check that all the adherent cells have been dislodged. If not, return them to the incubator for a few more minutes until detached.

steps.title: When cells have detached from the...
steps.instruction: When cells have detached from the base of the well, add 500 µL complete media to each well. Mix by pietting up and down using a P1000, and wash the base of the well ensuring cells are fully detached.

steps.title: Carefully aspirate the supernatent without disturbing the cell pellet
steps.instruction: Carefully aspirate the supernatent without disturbing the cell pellet. Resuspend each pellet in an appropriate volume of media to carry out either a 1:2 or 1:3 split ratio, dependent on growth rate. Discard extra cells.

steps.title: If cells are >90% confluent, passage...
steps.instruction: If cells are >90% confluent, passage at a 1:2 split ratio, following steps 7.1 - 7.5.

steps.title: Detach and collect cells following steps 7
steps.instruction: Detach and collect cells following steps 7.1 - 7.4. Carefully aspirate supernatent without disturbing the pellet.

steps.title: Collect the 1ml cell suspension in a labelled 1
steps.instruction: Collect the 1ml cell suspension in a labelled 1.5ml centrifuge tube and centrifuge 300 x g, °C, 3 minutes Centrifuge using a minifuge inside the MSC.

steps.title: Set up an experiment ensuring that...
steps.instruction: Set up an experiment ensuring that parameters FSC-A, FSC-W, SSC-A, 450/50-A (BFP) and 530/30-A (GFP) are selected.

steps.title: Run the mock sample first and...
steps.instruction: Run the mock sample first and use this to separate debris (FSC-A vs SSC-A) and identify single cells (FSC-A vs FSC-W). Use the polygon gate to select for this cells (see Figure 2). If required, adjust FSC and SSC voltages to ensure the main population lies within the plot. Figure 2. Setting up plots and gates for the mock sample.

steps.title: Check that the mock cells are...
steps.instruction: Check that the mock cells are negative for BFP and GFP using the filters: BFP 450/50 (405)-A and GFP 530/30 (488)-A and insert a polygon gate with the negative population positioned on the left side (Figure 2, bottom left panel). Run and record 10,000 events per sample.

steps.title: Next, run the control sample
steps.instruction: Next, run the control sample. The control sample should contain cells that are positive for both BFP and GFP. Run and record 10,000 events.

steps.title: Finally, run and record 10,000 events for the reporter sample
steps.instruction: Finally, run and record 10,000 events for the reporter sample.

steps.title: Export the
steps.instruction: Export the .fcs files. Carry out analysis using appropriate analysis software.

steps.title: Using the analysis software, start by analysing the mock sample
steps.instruction: Using the analysis software, start by analysing the mock sample. Firstly, identify single cells by drawing a gate around the main population in FSC-A vs SSC-A, as shown in Figure 2, top left panel. The following steps are for the use of FlowJo analysis software but can be adapted for use on other analysis software.

steps.title: Double-click inside the gate to open...
steps.instruction: Double-click inside the gate to open a new pane containing the selected cells. Identify single cells by drawing a gate around the population shown in Figure 2, top right panel.

steps.title: Double-click inside the gate to open...
steps.instruction: Double-click inside the gate to open a new pane containing single cells.

steps.title: Change the plot axis to show...
steps.instruction: Change the plot axis to show BFP vs SSC-A and draw a BFP+ gate based on the negative cells, see Figure 3, left panel. Cas9 activity is defined as the percentage of cells that have taken up the vector that have silenced GFP expression, so we need to identify BFP positive cells that are GFP negative. Figure 3. Setting BFP+ gates for the mock and control cells

steps.title: Copy these gates across to the...
steps.instruction: Copy these gates across to the control sample and check that it identifies BFP+ cells as shown in Figure 3, right panel.

steps.title: Copy all the gates over to the reporter sample
steps.instruction: Copy all the gates over to the reporter sample. Check that the Cas9 gate does not include any GFP+ cells. The majority of cells should have shifted to BFP+ GFP-. This will provide the percentage of cas9 positive cells in the cas9 cell line. Pass criteria: 75% cas9 positivity.

steps.title: Using the control cells, double-click on...
steps.instruction: Using the control cells, double-click on the BFP+ gate population and change the plot axis to BFP vs GFP as shown in Figure 4, left panel. Gate the region of BFP+ cells that are not GFP+. Name this gate Cas9. Figure 4. Identifying Cas9 positive cells
